title: Multiply and divide decimals by 10, 100, and 1000
description: Practice multiplying and dividing decimals by 10, 100, and 1000. For example, divide 31.4 by 100 to get 0.314.
topic_path: Multiplying and dividing decimals by 10, 100, and 1000
Multiply and divide decimals by 10, 100, and 1000

hints:
Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the left**.

Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the right**.

Number | Ten thousands | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$37$ |$\blueE{\leftarrow}$ | $\blueE{\leftarrow}$ | | $\blueE{\leftarrow}$ | $3$ | $7$
$37{,}000$ | $3$ | $7$ | $,$ | $0$ | $0$ | $0$

The digits in $37$ moved $3$ place values to the left. So we multiplied $37$ by $3$ factors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
37 \blueE{\times10 \times 10 \times 10} &=37{,}000 \\\\
37 \blueE{\times1{,}000} &= 37{,}000
\end{align}$

Number | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths 
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$37$ | $3$ | $7$ |  | $\maroonE{\rightarrow}$ | $\maroonE{\rightarrow}$ 
$0.37$  || $0$ |$.$ | $3$ | $7$

The digits in $37$ moved $2$ place values to the right. So we divided $37$ by $2$ divisors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
37 \maroonE{\div 10 \div 10 } &=0.37 \\\\
37 \maroonE{\div 100} &= 0.37
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
37 \blueE{\times1{,}000} &= 37{,}000 \\\\
37 \maroonE{\div 100} &= 0.37
\end{align}$

Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the left**.

Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the right**.

$9$ | $0$ | $9$ | $.$ | $0$ | $0$

$0.09 \blueE{\times100} = 9$

:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$0.09$ | $0$ | $.$ | $0$ | $9$ | $\maroonE{\rightarrow}$ 
$0.009$  | $0$ |$.$ | $0$ | $0$ | $9$

The digits in $0.09$ moved $1$ place value to the right. So we divided $0.09$ by $1$ divisor of $10$.

$0.09 \maroonE{\div 10 } =0.009$

$\begin{align}
0.09 \blueE{\times100} &= 9 \\\\
0.09 \maroonE{\div 10 } &=0.009
\end{align}$

Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the left**.

Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the right**.

\end{align}$
